summary: Matched the Target Test Luigi ground/stage file `grtluigi` by replacing `asm/melee/gr/grtluigi.s` with `src/melee/gr/grtluigi.c`, adding a new header, wiring the C object into `obj_files.mk`, and exposing supporting ground/display declarations. The PR also introduced a reusable `Ground_JObjInline1` inline in `ground.h` after review discussion showed that using new GObj getter helpers and C casts fixed stack/matching issues in repeated target-stage callbacks.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/gr/grtluigi.c` with C implementations for the stage callbacks and setup routines previously represented by assembly symbols such as `func_80221C10`, `lbl_80221C14`, `func_80221CB4`, `lbl_80221DD8`, and `lbl_80221E68`.
Added `src/melee/gr/grtluigi.h` declaring the grtluigi callback functions and setup function, using forward declarations for `HSD_GObj` and `HSD_JObj` plus `Vec3` from Dolphin matrix types.
Added `extern StageInfo stage_info;` and included `melee/gr/granime.h` in `ground.h` so the new C code could manipulate `stage_info.unk8C` and use ground animation/stage types.

Added a reusable static inline helper `Ground_JObjInline1(HSD_GObj* gobj)` in `ground.h`, which obtains `Map*` via `HSD_GObjGetUserData`, obtains `HSD_JObj*` via `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj`, then calls `func_801C2ED0(jobj, map->map_id)` and `func_801C8138(gobj, map->map_id, 0)`.
Added `#include <sysdolphin/baselib/forward.h>` to `grdisplay.h` so `HSD_GObj*` could be declared without requiring a full GObj definition.
Represented the stage callback table `lbl_803E8DF0` as a `StageCallbacks` array and the stage metadata `lbl_803E8E4C` as a `StageData` initializer for stage id `51` with asset path `"/GrTLg.dat"`.

Preserved behavior such as setting `stage_info.unk8C.b4 = false`, `stage_info.unk8C.b5 = true`, creating three stage GObjs via `func_80221CB4(0..2)`, registering GX links with `GObj_SetupGXLink`, and reporting failure with `OSReport("%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n", "grtluigi.c", 0xC3, arg0)`.
smart_moves: Factored the repeated JObj/map callback sequence into `Ground_JObjInline1` rather than keeping duplicate local code in `lbl_80221DD8` and `lbl_80221E68`.
Used `HSD_GObjGetUserData` and `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj` inside the new inline, matching review guidance that getter helpers plus casts could fix stack issues.

Kept the stage callback table and `StageData` layout close to the original assembly data, including callback ordering, `FLAGS_ZERO`, and the `(1 << 30) | (1 << 31)` flag value corresponding to `0xC0000000`.
Used a forward declaration include in `grdisplay.h` rather than pulling in a larger dependency just to declare `func_801C5DB0(HSD_GObj*, s32)`.
Converted the object file path in `obj_files.mk` in the same PR as the assembly removal, keeping the build graph consistent.
decomp_lessons: For target-stage ground files, repeated callback bodies that fetch `Map` and `HSD_JObj` from an `HSD_GObj` may require a shared inline using `HSD_GObjGetUserData` and `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj` to match stack layout.

Reviewer comments indicate that direct field access such as `gobj->user_data` and `gobj->hsd_obj` can leave unwanted stack differences in some matching contexts; the getter helpers and explicit C casts can be the better matching tactic.

If a decompiled stage file needs `stage_info`, add or expose the `extern StageInfo stage_info;` declaration in the appropriate shared ground header rather than locally inventing an incompatible declaration.

Stage callback arrays can be modeled directly with `StageCallbacks` entries, including empty callbacks, false-return callbacks, and packed flag constants, which helps translate data-section `.4byte` tables into searchable C.
When assembly contains embedded strings after stage data, a C `StageData` initializer can reference string literals such as `"/GrTLg.dat"` and the `OSReport` format string while preserving behavior.
Small no-op callbacks and bool callbacks should remain explicit functions when they are referenced from callback tables; deleting or merging them would obscure table identity and symbol matching.
naming_conventions: The PR retained unmatched symbol-style names such as `func_80221C10`, `lbl_80221C14`, `func_80221CB4`, and `lbl_803E8E4C` because no better names were evidenced.
The new file and header follow the stage module naming pattern `grtluigi.c` and `grtluigi.h` under `src/melee/gr/`.
The helper inline was named `Ground_JObjInline1`, using a `Ground_` prefix and a descriptive but still generic `JObjInline1` suffix because the exact semantic name was not established.
The stage asset path in `StageData` uses the game resource name `"/GrTLg.dat"`, while the source file is named `grtluigi.c`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the assembly object from `TEXT_O_FILES` and replaced it with the C object once the translation matched.
Translated `.data` callback table entries into a `StageCallbacks lbl_803E8DF0[4]` initializer and `StageData lbl_803E8E4C` initializer.
Used `Ground_JObjInline1(gobj)` in both `lbl_80221DD8` and `lbl_80221E68` to avoid manual unused-stack padding.
Review discussion specifically identified new GObj getters plus C casts as a way to fix stack output, including using the getter for `hsd_obj` as well as user data.

The intermediate issue noted in review was unused stack: an earlier draft had `#ifdef MUST_MATCH u8 unused[8]; #endif`, and the author observed a remaining 4 bytes of unused stack in commit `8d3fc0408ed0264d00ba599fec79e0b3cdb4fad3`.
For callbacks that simply return constants, the C translation used direct `return false;` and `return true;` forms matching the original `li r3, 0/1; blr` assembly pattern.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested fixing stack differences using the new GObj getters plus C casts.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Noted that the repeated operation is its own inline and appears frequently in target stages.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Observed that one tested commit still had 4 bytes of unused stack.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested using the getter for `hsd_obj` as well.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested using the inline in another repeated location.

follow_up_queries: Find other target stage files that duplicate the `func_801C2ED0` plus `func_801C8138` pattern and could use `Ground_JObjInline1`.
Search for remaining direct uses of `gobj->hsd_obj` or `gobj->user_data` in ground callback code where getter helpers may fix stack mismatches.
Check whether `grtlink`, `grtmewtwo`, or other neighboring target-stage assembly files use the same `StageCallbacks` and `StageData` structure pattern.
Review PR #699 for prerequisite header, getter, or stage-structure changes this PR depended on.
Review issue #598 to understand the original scope of unmatched `grtluigi` work.
